cases.name: Opera: 9...b5?? invites the Nxb5! demolition
cases.realCause: ASPIRATION CASE (expected to fail at baseline — feeds U2): the concrete cost of 9...b5 is 10.Nxb5! cxb5 11.Bxb5+ winning the attack and the pawn back with interest. Current comment leads with a development regression — true but not the reason.

cases.name: Opera: 7...Qe7 prepares Qb4+ — the engine-confirmed point must be named
cases.realCause: Qe7 is quiet; its engine-verified point is the follow-up Qb4+ forking king and bishop. A grounded comment names the prepared idea.

cases.name: Opera: 13.Rxd7! the rook sacrifice
cases.realCause: A true exchange sacrifice for the attack — the comment should say sacrifice-for-attack and stop; no spam on a famous brilliancy.

cases.name: Opera: 16.Qb8+!! the queen sacrifice
cases.realCause: The immortal queen sac forcing mate: only-move framing plus sacrifice is the full story in two lines.

cases.name: Opera: 17.Rd8# — checkmate needs one line
cases.realCause: Mate on the board. One sentence, nothing else to teach.

cases.name: Blackburne: 4.Nxe5? grabs the bait pawn — Qg5! is the point
cases.realCause: PARTIAL-CREDIT CASE: taking e5 walks into the gambit's point 4...Qg5! double-attacking g2 and the knight. Engine prefers 4.Nxd4. A center-regression lead is true but weak; the concrete answer (Qg5 or the safe Nxd4 capture) should carry the comment.

cases.name: Blackburne: 4...Qg5! — strong move, neutral silence is fine
cases.realCause: The trap move. With no clean single fact to voice, one quiet neutral line is correct — no lecture, no false praise of details.

cases.name: Blackburne: 5.Nxf7?? grabs at f7 while g2 burns
cases.realCause: g2 was already attacked; Nxf7 ignores it and 5...Qxg2 wins the rook by force (refutation walk: Qxg2 then Rf1 Qxe4+ etc.). The comment must name Qxg2.

cases.name: Blackburne: 5...Qxg2! — the only move that keeps the trap alive
cases.realCause: Critical-moment capture: it wins g2 for nothing and is the only move that holds the position together.

cases.name: Blackburne: 6...Qxe4+ — wins the pawn and shields h8
cases.realCause: Takes a free pawn with check while defending the h8 rook against Nxh8 — both points are board-verified.

cases.name: Blackburne: 7.Be2?? allows Nf3# (Qe2! was the quiet save)
cases.realCause: Be2 allows the smothered-style 7...Nf3#. The saving resource was the quiet 7.Qe2! pinning the e4 queen. The comment must lead with the allowed mate and name Nf3.

cases.name: Puzzle 000Pw: ...Ne2+! royal fork on queen and king
cases.realCause: Ne2+ forks Kg1 and Qc3 — the knight check wins the queen next move. The comment must say fork and name the loot.

cases.name: Puzzle 001cr: Be8# — bishop endgame mate in one
cases.realCause: Be8 is checkmate (g6 king boxed by Kf4 + g5 pawn + own pawns). One line.

cases.name: Puzzle 009FP: Bg5! the queen has nowhere good to go
cases.realCause: Bg5 hits the f6 queen which has no safe square (lichess theme: trappedPiece); after ...Qxg5 Qxg5 White wins the queen for a bishop.

cases.name: Puzzle 000Pw missed: ...Qd5? lets the royal fork slip
cases.realCause: Eval-verified: best is Ne2+ (royal fork, engine PV1 −542 white-POV); Qd5 is the engine's own PV2 at +245 — a swing of ~8 win-tiers. The comment must name the missed Ne2+ idea.

cases.name: CRAFTED TRAP: Rf1 lands on the open f-file — but the point is Rf8#, not the file
cases.realCause: THE rook case (from a real past failure): the rook move to the open file is right because it threatens Rf8# (Ba3 covers f8, g7/h7 seal the king) — the file is the road, the mate is the reason. Saying 'takes the open f-file' is true but teaches the wrong lesson.

cases.name: CRAFTED TRAP (Légal): ...Bxd1?? grabs the queen and gets mated
cases.realCause: The Légal trap: after 5.Nxe5 (a real queen offer), ...Bxd1?? takes the queen and loses to 6.Bxf7+ Ke7 7.Nd5#. Capturing the queen is the ultimate 'favorable exchange' bait — the comment must lead with the forced mate, and the capture may appear only as the failed idea.

cases.name: CRAFTED TRAP: Nf3 develops a piece — while the g5 bishop is left to die
cases.realCause: After 1.d4 d5 2.Bg5 h6, the bishop is attacked; 3.Nf3?? ignores it and ...hxg5 wins a piece. 'Develops the knight' is true and utterly beside the point — it may appear only inside the failed-idea frame, never as the visible verdict.
